content.full_text:
California
Fair POlitical
Practices Commission
February 22, L99O
Robert wagner
P. O. Box 425
South Pasadena, cA 91031
Re: Your Request for Advi.ce
our File No. A-90-078
Dear !1r. Wagner:
This is in response to your request for confirmation of
telephone advice concerning your duties and resPonsibilities under
the Political Reforn Act (the trActn.)r we only provide advice
related to the provisions of the Act. Please consult the
Secretary of State and local ordinances which roay impose ad-
ditional requirenents on your campaign for city council.
OUESTION
Is the name rrcoBmittee to Reelect
in compliance with the requirements of
Bob wagner to City Councilrr
the Act?
CONCLUSION
The name rrconmittee to Reelect Bob wagner to city Council'r is
in compliance with the reguirenents of the Act.
FACTS
You served as a member of the city council of the city of
Pasadena from 1984 througtr 1988. You have decided to campaign for
the same office in 1990. You are seeking our advice to deternine
whether use of the name rrcoumittee to Reelect Bob wagner to city
Councilrr would be in conpliance with the provisions of the Act.
During our telephone conversation of January l7 , 1990, I
advised you that the nane conplies with the requirements of the
Act. subsequently, I also nailed to you on the same date a copy
1 cor".n."nt code section 81ooo-91015. A11 statutory references
are to the GovernDent code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California code of Regulations Section
18OOO, et seo. A11 references to regnrlations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California code of Regulations.
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramenro CA 958O4-O8O7 . (916) 3 22.5660


Fi■e No. A-90-078
Page 2
Of
Act
and
ies of our Information Manual on Campai,qn
and Interim
ANALYSTS
The Act defines a cornmittee as any person or combination of
persons rrho directly or indirectly receives contributions totaling
one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more in a calendar year, 
(Sec-
tion 82013 (a) . ) Conmittees are required to file a statenent of
organization with the Secretary of State. (Section 84101(a).)
Additionally, a connittee which is controlLed directly or
indirectly by a candidate or which acts jointly with a candidate
in connection with the rnaking of expenditures is a controLled com-
rnittee. A candidate controls a conmittee if he, his agent, or any
other connittee he controls has a significant influence on the
actions or deci.sions of the comnittee. (Section 82016.)
The Coronission has interpreted the definition of r'controlled
committeerr very broadly to include any significant participation
in the actions of a coromj.ttee by a candidate, his or her agent, or
representatives of any other connittee he or she controls.
(Forcier Advice Letter, No. I-89-058, copy enclosed.)
The contents of the stateDent of organj,zation of a controlled
conmittee nust couply with specific reguirernents of the Act. 
Sec-
tion 84105, added to the Act by Proposition 68, has been
determined to be not in conflict rrith Proposition 73 and severabLe
from the other provisions of Proposition 68. (In re Be11 (1988)
11 FPPC Ops. 1, copy enclosed.) It requires that the name of a
coDmittee include or be acconpanied by the name of any individual,
entity or other person controlling the coDmittee. (See also
Baqatelos Advice Letter, No. I-88-475, copy enclosed.)
The nane ttconnittee to Reelect Bob Wagner to City Council'l
includes your name as required by Section 84105. Accordingly, it
is in conpliance with the requirenents of the Act. The fact that
you are not currently a member of the city council has no bearing
on our conclusion. The Act does not regulate that aspect of your
committee, s nane.
Should you have any further questions regarding this Eatter
do not hesitate to contact ne at (916) 322-5907.
S incerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel
